intent: Fix a Python-version-fragile code_tutor test so testsAndMisc CI (Python 3.11) passes: the codebase_fingerprint OSError-branch test passed on 3.14 but failed on 3.11.
scope: Non-goal: any change to _analyzer.py source (behavior unchanged).
changes: test_codebase_fingerprint_stat_oserror_raises now patches Path.is_file to return True for the target file (without calling stat) in addition to patching Path.stat to raise, so the loop deterministically reaches the guarded path.stat() call and hits the `except OSError` branch on every Python version.

Root cause: on 3.11/3.12 Path.is_file() calls stat() and re-raises OSErrors without a recognized errno, so the injected error escaped before the guarded stat() was reached; on 3.14 is_file() uses os.path.isfile and did not.

verification.evidence: 1 passed on 3.11.11 (the CI version that previously failed)
1 passed on 3.14; _analyzer.py lines 386-387 (except OSError branch) covered

risks: Patching is_file globally could mask a real is_file bug, but the mock delegates to the real is_file for all non-target paths.
rollback: git revert the commit; the prior test still covers the branch on 3.14 only.
After rollback, re-run on 3.11 to confirm the flake returns.
